source.title: Hippocampo-cortical coupling mediates memory consolidation during sleep

confidence_rationale: Closed-loop causal manipulation of the coordination between hippocampal sharp-wave ripples, cortical delta waves, and cortical spindles in rats. Reinforcing the endogenous phase relationship reorganises medial prefrontal cortical networks specifically and rescues performance on a spatial memory task that otherwise does not consolidate. Directly supports the MECH-261 claim that mode-specific carrier-rhythm phase coupling selects mPFC as the write target. Mapping to REE's SD-033a lateral-PFC-analog is strong because the rodent mPFC Maingret record from is the closest functional homologue of SD-033a in the rodent literature.
failure_signatures: Control rats receiving the same number of stimulation events but without respecting the endogenous SWR-delta-spindle coupling geometry performed at chance. A MECH-261 implementation that induces write events without the phase relation will fail to consolidate.

The effect was observed on mPFC activity specifically -- other cortical regions were not reorganised by the manipulation. A write-gate that fires non-selectively across cortical subdivisions contradicts this selectivity.

The task was designed to trigger encoding but not consolidation under sham conditions; the hippocampo-cortical coupling was the switch that converted encoding-without-consolidation into consolidation-with-recall. If MECH-261 is ablated in REE, encoding-phase residues should persist at episode start but fail to transfer into the lateral-PFC write target even after nominal SWS passes.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Fine-grained temporal coordination between hippocampal sharp-wave ripples, cortical delta waves, and cortical spindles during NREM sleep is causally required for consolidation of weakly-encoded spatial memories, and reinforcing this coordination selectively reorganises medial prefrontal cortical networks.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-261's dict-keyed write-gate is, in SWS, implemented by hippocampal ripples that land on the cortical delta+spindle phase window selectively over sites that the hippocampus-to-cortex projection reaches. In rodents this is mPFC. In REE, SD-033a is the lateral-PFC-analog write target; the Maingret causal demonstration is the tightest behavioural-level evidence that the write gate is both phase-selected and cortical-site-specific.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Rodent mPFC spans prelimbic and infralimbic areas; the mapping to the primate lateral-PFC functional role that SD-033 cluster draws from (Rushworth, Boorman, Koechlin) is approximate. Maingret's primary evidence is for the rodent hippocampo-mPFC dialog; its direct translation to SD-033a is subject to the broader rodent-primate PFC-subdivision homology assumption documented in REE_assembly/docs/architecture/sd_033_pfc_subdivision_architecture.md.
mapping.source_context: Adult male Long-Evans rats; custom spatial memory task designed to trigger encoding but not consolidation; closed-loop stimulation triggered on hippocampal ripples and phased to cortical delta-wave detection during post-training NREM; multi-site LFP + unit recordings in mPFC.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality very high (Nature Neuroscience, causal closed-loop manipulation, proper control). Mapping fidelity high because Maingret explicitly demonstrate cortical-site-specific reorganisation (mPFC), which is the gated-target property MECH-261 predicts. Transfer risk moderate: rodent mPFC to SD-033a lateral-PFC-analog mapping carries the known homology cost that the SD-033 design doc already flags.
